title: So lets swap Komm susser tod for Tsubasa wo Kudasai
display_title: So lets swap Komm susser tod for Tsubasa wo Kudasai
wikitext:
[]: So let's swap Komm susser tod for Tsubasa wo Kudasai!             Quote Post by John » Sat Oct 08, 2011 12:08 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=259OyppZptY It ruins the whole "Komm susser todd" experience. But I love some of those abysmal musical coincidences at the beginning. Sadly by the end it's just a loop... (Oh my god why didn't I notice those erratic violins by the end of Tsubasa wo Kudasai too?)

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 08, 2011 12:40 pm this makes me remember just how violent the imagery in EoE is.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Sat Oct 08, 2011 3:27 pm It makes me feel like the third impact is a GOOD THING.

[]:             Quote Post by liquidus118 » Sat Oct 08, 2011 5:05 pm Has anyone tried swapping K, ST with Everything You've Ever Dreamed? The original song they were going to use before replacing it with K, ST IIRC.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Oct 08, 2011 5:14 pm liquidus118 wrote: Has anyone tried swapping K, ST with Everything You've Ever Dreamed? The original song they were going to use before replacing it with K, ST IIRC.

[]:             Quote Post by Stryker » Sat Oct 08, 2011 5:15 pm What about this song?

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sat Oct 08, 2011 5:25 pm Not that I know of, But seriously... I think it would sound ridiculously odd... "Everything you've ever dreamed" sounds so different to other NGE themes. Something I've noticed in some Eva OSTs is how the Full Songs are so different to the songs presents in the series/movies. Ever wondered why "Komm Susser Todd Director's Cut "? or Why Robe des Champs is so different to the one in the movie? Like if Anno never liked Shiro's first songs editions/mixes (whatever I should call it )...

[]:             Quote Post by MandarinOrange » Sat Oct 08, 2011 11:52 pm I rather liked how Tsubasa wo Kudasai worked with the scene. But, how about the end of the world to "I Melt With You"? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Du7M1TBZObo

[]:             Quote Post by Aiko Heiwa » Sun Oct 09, 2011 12:00 am MandarinOrange wrote: I rather liked how Tsubasa wo Kudasai worked with the scene. But, how about the end of the world to "I Melt With You"? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Du7M1TBZObo

[]:             Quote Post by John » Sun Oct 09, 2011 11:56 am MandarinOrange wrote: how about the end of the world to "I Melt With You"? http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Du7M1TBZObo

[]:             Quote Post by Tsuki No Nagisa » Sun Oct 23, 2011 12:05 pm Actually , it ruins the concept of "Shinji is singing komm susser tod (the lyrics are his feelings)" , For a troll scene like that (mostly ripping off lilith) i would use something like um... ! Kyou no hi wa sayonara! In Komm susser tod scenes , Shinji just smiled when he saw Kaworu. lol

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Mon Oct 24, 2011 11:16 pm In some very weird way, "Tsubasa no Kudasai" works here for me. I know there's a deeper meaning behind the original song, and "Komm Susser Todd" is the better of the two songs, but I wouldn't be too objectionable to "Tsubasa"

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Tue Oct 25, 2011 12:10 am A Night on Bald Mountain works better, starting from the beginning of the kitchen scene. It completely changes the mood of the scene, but it remains appropriate.

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Tue Oct 25, 2011 12:28 am OH GOD. [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ccue0DVUx_w[/url] Not sure whatever this is awesome or HORROR. (but it kind of fits)

[]:             Quote Post by tomrule123 » Wed Oct 26, 2011 9:32 am I wonder... what if we swap "Komm Susser Todd" with "Hey Jude" from "The Beatles".... NAH! Best not to mess around with this song. Obviously, they have different context when comparing the songs. ... unless we use "Revolution 9". GOD, WHY THE HELL AM I THINKING ABOUT THIS?! Bad idea to use Revolution 9. Better switch over to Pink Floyd. WAIT a minute... I wonder...

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Oct 26, 2011 3:09 pm ^Nah, let's switch it with Cowboy Bebop songs like "Call Me, Call Me" and "Blue" instead.

[]:             Quote Post by Seen » Wed Oct 26, 2011 11:29 pm Someone wrote: [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ccue0DVUx_w[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Someone » Thu Oct 27, 2011 1:15 am Seen wrote: OH FUCK MY SIDES

[]:             Quote Post by Dirty Mick » Fri Oct 28, 2011 10:59 am I'm still getting the same apocalyptic depressing feeling from this one as I did from the other.
summary:
[]: So let's swap Komm susser tod for Tsubasa wo Kudasai!             Quote Post by John » Sat Oct 08, 2011 12:08 pm http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=259OyppZptY It ruins the whole "Komm susser todd" experience. But I love some of those abysmal musical coincidences at the beginning. Sadly by the end it's just a loop... (Oh my god why didn't I notice those erratic violins by the end of Tsubasa wo Kudasai too?)
